textPlain: I will never forget what it was like to grow up in the 80s.  As a child often I was at some kind of child care. After school programs, or a neighbors house. I remember what it was like, wanting my parents to pick me up. I lived in a place that was surrounded by bullies and abusive religious zealots.  Often times I would be staring out the window or looking towards the road wondering when my loving parents would come and get me and take me away from the nightmare situation.  Endless hours staring, wondering, hoping. Praying for them to pick me up and take me away.And that is why I happily allow my wife and my children to track me at all times.So they will never feel that kind of pain and despair that a young child once felt.

Is there some reason why everyone trying to give advice is "a victim of abuse" or "coming from an abused family". Bro, I don't need all your neurotic ass advice. Your life sucked. I get it. It's unfortunate and I wish it were not that way. But that's why you shouldn't be giving advice. Y'all are broken people.Been sharing location for years. 20+ people. My wife and I share our location with our friends. My parents share theirs with our friends. My friends have my door code and keys and I have theirs. We stop by every now and then in the city of San Francisco with a quick text that looks like this "hey, walking by, mind if I pop in?"You guys will spend half your living breath talking about your neuroses needing to be widespread positions and the other half talking about a loneliness epidemic. These are both the same thing.
i mean this is cute in an isolated incident, until, you know, all the corporations sell that info to a fascist government who uses it to track dissidents in Portalnd.
